title: Frontend WebSocket — Shared Definitions
description: Shared $defs reused across the frontend WebSocket frame schemas: the connectivity enum, the activity-feed event kind/severity enums, and the metric→unit-bound sensor reading and actuator sample carried by telemetry frames. Mirrors the conventions of contracts/mqtt and contracts/frontend-rest (RFC-007 units, closed enums); kept local rather than cross-contract $ref'd, matching frontend-rest.

connectivity.description: A greenhouse's controller connectivity as the platform sees it: online = fresh telemetry; degraded = a non-critical fault or stale stream; offline = no contact. Offline is data absence, not a fault. The same enum the frontend-rest contract carries.

event_kind.description: Closed set of activity-feed event kinds surfaced to the operator (frontend data model §4). Matches the frontend-rest EventEntry kind.

event_severity.description: The platform's dashboard grading: info = informational; warning = degraded but operating; critical = raises a toast. Distinct from the controller's fault severity (warning/alarm).

metric.description: Closed set of measured/derived quantities (RFC-007), matching the MQTT sensor-reading contract. vpd is derived from temperature + humidity.

unit.description: Unit of measure. Bound to the metric by the conditional clauses in `reading` (RFC-007 units convention).

reading.description: One metric sample in a telemetry frame: a value with its unit bound to the metric, the same metric→unit binding the MQTT sensor-reading contract enforces. The sample time is the frame envelope `ts`.

value.description: Numeric reading in the unit fixed for this metric.

actuator_name.description: Closed set of actuators (physical-system-single.md §Outputs); the same names the MQTT, controller-rest, and frontend-rest contracts use. Kept local rather than cross-contract $ref'd, matching this contract's convention.

actuator_sample.description: One actuator sample in a telemetry frame: commanded vs observed position (the output-side channel, controller safety §5). Mirrors the frontend-rest ActuatorState, with the sample time carried by the frame envelope `ts`. Position is a 0–100 number: variable/modulating actuators report their level (% open / speed / dim); pure on/off devices (misters, co2_injector, irrigation_valve) report 0 (off) or 100 (on) — the platform's flattening of the MQTT {on, level_pct} shape.

commanded.description: Commanded position/level for this sample (0–100; on/off devices use 0=off, 100=on).

observed.description: Observed (readback) position (0–100), or null when the readback is unavailable.

time_scale.description: Simulation-only: the greenhouse controller's current simulated-clock speed multiplier (1.0 = real-time; <1 slow-motion; >1 fast-forward). Each controller's clock is independent. Drives the dashboard's per-greenhouse speed indicator; absent on real-hardware controllers. Mirrors the controller's `system-state.simulation.time_scale` and the controller-rest `TimeScale.scale` (controller HAL §7).
